page: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v6 is a member of the Dlsv6 server family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a dedicated clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. Cache resources consist of 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. Memory allocation is 32.0 GB, which translates to a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2104.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.3 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15551.09 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13049.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 1229.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 122.61 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 348.14 tokens/sec, though text generation drops to 15.33 tokens/sec with a medium 7B model.

The qualitative cost profile of the D16ls_v6 reflects its low-memory design. By limiting RAM to 2.0 GB per vCPU and omitting local storage, the instance reduces resource density costs for workloads that require high CPU clock speeds but have modest memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage and the low memory capacity, which limits its utility for large in-memory databases or massive datasets. This server is designed for compute-bound workloads with low memory requirements, such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a dedicated clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2104.0 and a top-tier text generation speed of 348.14 tokens/sec for small LLM models. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This server is designed for compute-heavy tasks with low memory requirements, such as software compilation, text processing, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 32.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for low-memory compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v6 is an Intel-based virtual machine with 16 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: An Intel-powered compute instance with high clock speeds and a low-memory footprint for efficient CPU-bound processing.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz dedicated clock speed
32.0 GB of RAM providing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core
No local storage included, but configured for Premium Storage compatibility
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language model inference
Designed for software compilation, web serving, and compute-bound workloads
